LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF GEORGIA HERDENER'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS FILED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S AND NON-PARTY WITNESS PAUL BOWEN'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS FILED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SEEMS TO BE UNDER MISCONCEPTIONS THAT SEALING MATERIAL FORECLOSES THE COURT FROM CONSIDERING IT AND THAT A SEALING ORDER FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MATERIALS APPLIES THROUGH TRIAL; NEITHER IS SO. COUNSEL FOR MOVANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiffs and Non-Party Witness Paul Bowens' Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Plaintiffs Opposition To And In Support Of Defendants Reply In Support Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs:

GRANTED. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeremy Pasternak, Esq. 415-693-0300 for Plaintiff.
Natalie Pierce, Esq. 415-519-3008 of Littler Mendelson P.C. for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court admonishes Plaintiff re: contesting late. Plaintiff argues and directs the Court's attention to Plaintiff's exhibit F. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Tapjoy, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted.

Defendants' defense of after-acquired evidence does not bar Plaintiff's claims, as it only limits Plaintiff's remedies, rather than disposing of an entire cause of action, as is required for summary adjudication. Under Cal. Civ. Proc. 437c(f)(1), a "motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty." The doctrines of after-acquired evidence and unclean hands are "not complete defenses to a worker's claims under California's FEHA, although they do affect the availability of remedies." (Salas v. Sierra Chemical Co. (2014) 59 Cal.4th 407, 414; see also Horne v. District Council 16 Internat. Union of Painters & Allied Trades (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 524, 537-541[trial court erred in relying upon after-acquired evidence in granting summary judgment for employer in employment discrimination action].)

Plaintiff's and non-party witness Paul Bowen's motion to seal documents filed in support of defendant's motion for summary adjudication is GRANTED. Counsel for movants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. The order must also specify the title of the documents to be sealed, as reflected on the Court's register of action, and the date those documents were filed.
